programmes.show_description: As whales attack tourists in Canada and lobsters turn deadly in France, a scientist discovers aggressive worms multiplying at speed. (S1, ep 2)
A trail of blood leaves officers puzzled, a man high on drink and drugs launches a tirade of abuse, and a domestic fight leads to an arrest. (S5, ep 8)
Documentary series charting the exploits of the City of London police, charged with safeguarding the Square Mile at the heart of the crime-ridden capital. (S4, ep 1)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 5)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 6)
Staff at Auckland Airport meet a Greek man with no money but a rich history, while Customs find jeans with more than just dodgy labels to hide. (S11, ep 9)
Customs deal with a Lithuanian man who has very questionable holiday plans and a risky item lands at the International Mail Centre. (S11, ep 10)
The Highway Cops step in when a reckless teen's speeding ends badly and a caravan loses a part at the top of the Lindis Pass. (S4, ep 3)
A fisherman finds himself in hot water and so does a drunk driver who can barely stand, as the Highway Cops continue their patrol of New Zealand's busiest roads. (S4, ep 4)
Teleshopping.

Documentary series from New Zealand following the madness and mayhem faced by intrepid officers as they pull over motorists for a variety of suspected offences. (S17, ep 1)

A teen's summer plans hit a roadblock and luxury items prove even more costly for a forgetful shopper. Meanwhile, a vagabond musician must prove his worth. (S2, ep 20)
A gun-carrying pleasure boat is examined by the marine team. Meanwhile, an unwise relationship could cause heartache for a would-be tourist. (S2, ep 21)

Blood Oath: Three aged Klingon warriors arrive to fulfil a blood oath they made with Dax decades ago, to kill a man known as the Albino.
2010: Ten years have passed since an alien race known as the Aschen saved Earth and cured cancer but Carter discovers the awful truth behind this seemingly infallible race. (S4, ep 16)
Rising (Part 2 of 2): Unable to return to Earth, the crew looks for a new fuel source. They happen upon a settlement, but the villagers live in fear. The enemy is coming. (S1, ep 2)
The Arsenal of Freedom: The Enterprise travels to the legendary world of Minosa, but the crew find themselves under attack by a highly sophisticated weapons system.
Follow Australian Customs and Border Protection officers as they attempt to enforce the country's quarantine, customs, immigration and finance laws. (S16, ep 5)
Follow Australian Customs and Border Protection officers as they attempt to enforce the country's quarantine, customs, immigration and finance laws. (S16, ep 6)
William Devin Howell: Take an in-depth look at the life and crimes of William Devin Howell who was found guilty of the murders of seven different women. (S9, ep 2)
Sinead Wooding: The story of a young wife and mother-of-four, murdered ferociously by her controlling husband and an accomplice.
Dear Doctor: The crew discovers a new planet with two races, one in desperate need of medical and scientific assistance. (S1, ep 13)
Venice sees an unprecedented plague of jellyfish, while in Canada, researcher Anawak is getting to the bottom of whale attacks. (S1, ep 3)
Northumbria Police answer a flood of 999 calls, while officers raid a property they suspect is being used by men to exploit young girls. (S5, ep 9)

Biosecurity officer Jade makes an expensive witchcraft-related discovery. Also, some suspicious lampshades reach Customs. (S12, ep 1)
Immigration discovers an attempt to establish a dangerous and illegal business in New Zealand. Meanwhile, Customs uncovers a drugs scam. (S12, ep 2)
A dust cloud wreaks havoc on the Canterbury Plains and the cops sniff out some diesel fraud. (S4, ep 5)
A driver having a little too much fun on a South Island road trip is caught downing beers, and a couple on their honeymoon have trouble keeping their eyes on the road. (S4, ep 6)

A British man wants to close the book on his failed Canadian marriage. Meanwhile, an old charge could end a budding romance. (S2, ep 22)
A giant x-ray machine finds an odd shape in a shipping container. At the mail centre, meanwhile, a strange declaration leads to contraband. (S2, ep 23)

The Maquis Part 1: When a Cardassian freighter is destroyed as it departs DS9, the finger of suspicion points at Federation colonists who now live in Cardassian territory.
Absolute Power: SG-1 notice a change in Daniel Jackson after he is reunited with the Harsesis child, Shifu (Lane Gates). (S4, ep 17)
Hide And Seek: A game causes one of the Athosian children to open a unit and unleash a dark entity. They soon realise that the harmless-looking cloud is anything but... (S1, ep 3)
Symbiosis: Picard has to decide whether to continue to supply a civilisation with a drug they have become dependent on, after they initially had to take it to combat a plague 200 years ago.

Murder in Suburbia: 35-year-old Zara Aleena was beaten to death just ten minutes from her home. The only evidence of the killer was one bloody fingerprint. (S3, ep 2)
Murder on the Moor: In 1975, school girl Lesley Molseed went to the shops and never came home. Could one 30-year-old piece of evidence reveal her killer? (S3, ep 3)
Sleeping Dogs: When the crew enter a wounded Klingon vessel, they are ambushed by a Klingon and left stranded on the sinking ship with Archer their only hope for survival. (S1, ep 14)
